name: Futures & Promises
def: A placeholder for a value that will be available later, with the producer side called a promise.
problem: Represent and compose results of asynchronous computations without blocking threads.
mechanism: A future is read-only and completes when its promise is fulfilled; callbacks/combinators (then/map) chain work; await suspends until ready.
limits: Eager vs lazy semantics differ by language
failures: Forgotten error handling on a future
Blocking on a future from its own executor → deadlock
myths: "Future = thread" — a future is a value handle; it may run on a pool, inline, or lazily
